content.text: A nested chat is a complete sub-conversation that fires automatically inside another agent's reply loop when a specific trigger condition is met. The outer conversation never sees the sub-conversation turns — it only receives the final summarised reply from the inner chat. Use nested chats to delegate specialised work (code execution, research, review) without exposing the inner back-and-forth to the main conversation.

attrs.alt: AutoGen nested chat: trigger fires when outer user proxy receives a matching message, inner code_specialist and code_executor run privately, final result injected into outer conversation

content.text: to attach a nested chat to any
. When a message received by that agent matches the trigger, AutoGen runs the nested conversations in
order and injects the final reply back.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

# Outer orchestrator
orchestrator = AssistantAgent(
    "orchestrator",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You manage tasks. Delegate coding to specialists. Say TERMINATE when done.",
)

# Inner agents (for the nested chat)
code_specialist = AssistantAgent(
    "code_specialist",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write and debug Python code. Say TERMINATE when done.",
)
code_executor = UserProxyAgent(
    "code_executor",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "nested_ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

# Attach the nested chat to the orchestrator:
# fires when a message the orchestrator RECEIVES contains "code request"
orchestrator.register_nested_chats(
    chat_queue=[
        {
            "sender": code_executor,
            "recipient": code_specialist,
            "message": lambda sender, recipient, context: context.get("last_message", {}).get("content", ""),
            "max_turns": 6,
            "summary_method": "last_msg",
        }
    ],
    trigger=lambda sender: "code request" in (
        sender.last_message(orchestrator) or {}
    ).get("content", "").lower(),
)

outer_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "outer_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config=False,
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)
outer_proxy.initiate_chat(
    orchestrator,
    message="I have a code request: write a function that computes factorial recursively.",
)
Trigger is called on the SENDER of the incoming message:
the trigger lambda receives the agent that sent the message to the nested chat host (the orchestrator in this example). Access the message content via
list can contain multiple conversation specs. AutoGen runs them sequentially, passing the summary of each as
context to the next. This lets a nested sequence do multiple sub-tasks (research, then implement, then review) all within one outer reply slot.

key in each chat spec determines what is sent to start the nested conversation. It can be a static string, a callable, or a dict. The callable form is the most flexible — use it to pass context from the triggering message.
The summary of the nested chat is what gets injected back into the outer conversation. Choose the right method to control how verbose that summary is.
The final message from the inner conversation
The LLM writes a condensed summary of the whole inner chat

Agents in a group chat can also have nested chats registered. When the coder agent in a group chat receives a message matching the trigger, it runs a private code execution sub-conversation and returns the result — other group chat agents only see the final answer.

from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

coder = AssistantAgent("coder", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You are a coder. When you receive a task, write Python code.")

# Private code execution sub-agents for the coder
exec_specialist = AssistantAgent("exec_specialist", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Execute and debug Python code. Say TERMINATE when done.")
executor = UserProxyAgent("executor", human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "nested_gc", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))

# Register nested chat ON the coder agent
coder.register_nested_chats(
    chat_queue=[{
        "sender": executor,
        "recipient": exec_specialist,
        "message": lambda s, r, ctx: ctx.get("last_message", {}).get("content", ""),
        "max_turns": 5,
        "summary_method": "last_msg",
    }],
    trigger=lambda sender: True,   # always fire — every message to coder triggers nested chat
)

researcher = AssistantAgent("researcher", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="Research topics. Be concise.")
outer_proxy = UserProxyAgent("outer_proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config=False,
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))

gc = GroupChat(agents=[outer_proxy, researcher, coder], messages=[], max_round=8)
mgr = GroupChatManager(groupchat=gc, llm_config=llm_config)
outer_proxy.initiate_chat(mgr, message="Research quicksort and implement it.")
Verify that the nested chat fires and returns the expected summary by registering a mock for the inner agent and asserting the outer conversation received the nested result.

import pytest
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

def test_nested_chat_injects_result():
    llm_config = False

    orchestrator = AssistantAgent("orchestrator", llm_config=llm_config,
        human_input_mode="NEVER")
    orchestrator.register_reply(
        trigger=None,
        reply_func=lambda r, msgs, s, cfg: (True, "I'll delegate. TERMINATE"),
        position=0,
    )

    # Inner specialist returns a canned reply
    inner_specialist = AssistantAgent("inner_specialist", llm_config=False,
        human_input_mode="NEVER")
    inner_specialist.register_reply(
        trigger=None,
        reply_func=lambda r, msgs, s, cfg: (True, "code result: 42 TERMINATE"),
        position=0,
    )
    inner_proxy = UserProxyAgent("inner_proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
        code_execution_config=False,
        is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))

    # Register nested chat: fires on any message with "code request"
    orchestrator.register_nested_chats(
        [{
            "sender": inner_proxy,
            "recipient": inner_specialist,
            "message": "Run the task.",
            "max_turns": 2,
            "summary_method": "last_msg",
        }],
        trigger=lambda s: "code request" in (s.last_message(orchestrator) or {}).get("content", ""),
    )

    outer_proxy = UserProxyAgent("outer", human_input_mode="NEVER",
        code_execution_config=False,
        is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))
    result = outer_proxy.initiate_chat(orchestrator, message="I have a code request.", max_turns=3)
    assert result.summary is not None
Nested chats are private sub-conversations that fire when an agent receives a matching message; the outer conversation only sees the final summary.
Register with agent.register_nested_chats(chat_queue=[{sender, recipient, message, max_turns, summary_method}], trigger=...).
The trigger callable receives the sender of the triggering message and returns True to fire the nested chat.
chat_queue can contain multiple specs that run sequentially with carryover — useful for research → implement → review pipelines.
summary_method controls what is injected back: 'last_msg' (free), 'reflection_with_llm' (LLM call), or a custom function.
Agents inside group chats can have nested chats registered — other group members only see the final result.
Test by mocking the inner specialist's reply and asserting the outer conversation received the nested result in its summary.
